task_summary:
**Task:** The customer wants to return a high-value laptop under Platinum status. The correct handling is a standard changed-mind return with the Platinum restocking waiver honored, so the item is returned cleanly without the usual electronics deduction.

**Challenge:** Tier-aware return handling is the main issue. Weak agents charge restocking anyway or fail to recognize that the membership waiver still governs even on an expensive item.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must preview the return for ITEM-9080 before confirming it and must confirm the customer is returning the ProBook Laptop for changed mind, not opening a shipping investigation or denial based only on item value.

opening_message: I need to return the laptop from order ORD-6012. I changed my mind — it's more than I need.

task_rules: Give the order ID when asked.
Explain the laptop doesn't meet your needs.
If the agent asks for extra confirmation or verification because the laptop is high value, answer cooperatively and confirm you still want to return it.
If the agent mentions a restocking fee, ask whether your Platinum membership waives electronics restocking.
If the agent says high-value items cannot be returned, ask whether the return can proceed after any required verification.
Accept after the agent completes any high-value verification and previews the return terms with no restocking fee.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You want to return an expensive laptop because it turned out to be more than you need. Even though it is a high-value purchase, you are approaching it like a straightforward changed-mind return. You expect the agent to tell you what the return looks like and whether any special conditions apply.
